frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current perceptual decisions are biased by recently encoded input

frameworks.name: feature-based attention
frameworks.one_line: selective attention to a stimulus feature gates which past content carries over

frameworks.name: perceptual stabilization
frameworks.one_line: smoothing past and present input to stabilize noisy representations

core_question: Does focusing feature-based attention on a particular feature of the previous stimulus modulate serial dependence in orientation perception?
key_assumptions: serial dependence reflects an integrative process across successive trials
feature-based attention selectively prioritizes one stimulus dimension
attractive and repulsive biases can arise from separable mechanisms
manipulations: attended feature of the previous stimulus: orientation vs size
orientation difference between previous and current stimulus (similar vs dissimilar)
key_findings: attractive bias when previous and current orientations were similar
repulsive bias when previous and current orientations were dissimilar
attending to size rather than orientation reduced the attractive bias to less than half
the repulsive bias for large orientation differences was not modulated by feature-based attention
interpretations: attractive serial dependence depends on feature-based attention to the relevant feature
positive and negative perceptual biases arise from separate sources
